name: Supergravity & UV Behavior of Perturbative Gravity

concepts.name: Perturbative non-renormalizability of gravity
concepts.definition: Because Newton's constant has negative mass dimension ([G]=M^-2), the superficial degree of divergence grows with loop order (D=2L+2 in d=4), requiring infinitely many independent counterterms of ever higher curvature dimension and destroying predictivity in the UV.

concepts.name: Goroff-Sagnotti two-loop counterterm
concepts.definition: Nonzero two-loop divergence of pure Einstein gravity proportional to the cube of the Weyl/Riemann tensor (C^3) with coefficient 209/2880; it cannot be removed by field redefinition or equations of motion, definitively proving perturbative non-renormalizability.

concepts.name: Gravity as an effective field theory (Donoghue)
concepts.definition: General relativity treated as a low-energy EFT: unknown UV physics is absorbed into renormalized coefficients of a local Lagrangian, while nonanalytic terms (ln, sqrt of momentum) give universal, parameter-independent low-energy quantum predictions such as the leading correction to the Newtonian potential.

concepts.name: Nonanalytic quantum corrections
concepts.definition: Parts of loop amplitudes that are nonanalytic in momentum transfer (e.g. sqrt(-q^2), ln(-q^2)); they dominate at long distance, are insensitive to UV counterterms, and yield reliable, UV-completion-independent predictions in the EFT of gravity.

concepts.name: Quantum correction to the Newtonian potential
concepts.definition: Leading genuine quantum (hbar) correction to V(r) computed in the EFT of gravity, with universal coefficient 41/(10 pi); accompanied by the classical post-Newtonian 3G(m1+m2)/(rc^2) term. Too small to measure but a mandatory IR check for any quantum gravity theory.

concepts.name: Supergravity (N=1..8)
concepts.definition: Locally supersymmetric extension of general relativity with N supercharges and a gravitino (spin 3/2). Higher N gives stronger UV cancellations; N=8 is maximal in 4D since more supersymmetry would force spins greater than 2.

concepts.name: N=8 supergravity
concepts.definition: Maximal 4D supergravity (toroidal reduction of 11D supergravity) with 1 graviton, 8 gravitinos, 28 vectors, 56 fermions, 70 scalars; hidden global E7(7) and local SU(8) symmetry. Candidate for a perturbatively UV-finite point-particle quantum gravity.

concepts.name: E7(7) duality symmetry
concepts.definition: Exceptional continuous global symmetry of N=8 supergravity that constrains allowed UV counterterms; F-term counterterms cannot be E7(7)-invariant below seven loops, so the lowest admissible invariant counterterm (partial^6 R^4) appears only at L=7.

concepts.name: Enhanced UV cancellations
concepts.definition: UV cancellations in supergravity amplitudes that go beyond what all known symmetries and superspace power-counting predict (e.g. N=5 finite at 4 loops, N=8 better than expected at 3-5 loops). No symmetry-based explanation currently exists.

concepts.name: Critical dimension D_c(L) for N=8 UV finiteness
concepts.definition: Spacetime dimension below which the L-loop four-point amplitude of N=8 supergravity is finite: D_c=6 (3 loops), 11/2 (4 loops), 24/5 (5 loops). The relation D_c=4+6/L (the N=4 SYM bound) holds only through four loops; at five loops D_c=24/5 lies below the 26/5 that formula would give, but Bern et al. (2018) show 24/5 is exactly the standard D^8R^4 / E7(7) prediction (no enhanced cancellation at five loops). D=4 is reached at L=7, making the seventh loop critical for the 4D finiteness question.

concepts.name: N=5 supergravity four-loop finiteness
concepts.definition: N=5 supergravity is UV finite at four loops despite supersymmetry and duality arguments predicting a divergence; the cancellation is an enhanced cancellation, contrasting with N=4 which diverges at four loops via a U(1) duality anomaly.

concepts.name: Double copy / KLT / BCJ color-kinematics duality
concepts.definition: Construction expressing gravity amplitudes as a 'square' of gauge-theory amplitudes via the KLT momentum kernel; BCJ color-kinematics duality (kinematic Jacobi identity) extends this to loop level, enabling otherwise impossible multiloop supergravity integrand computations. N=8 SUGRA = (N=4 SYM)^2.

concepts.name: Generalized unitarity method
concepts.definition: On-shell amplitude technique that reconstructs loop integrands by sewing tree amplitudes on multiple cuts; together with color-kinematics duality and the double copy it powers state-of-the-art supergravity UV computations.

concepts.name: Eleven-dimensional supergravity
concepts.definition: Unique maximal supergravity (Cremmer-Julia-Scherk 1978); the highest dimension supporting a consistent supergravity with spin <=2. Its dimensional reduction yields 4D N=8 supergravity, and it is the low-energy limit of M-theory.

concepts.name: Higher-derivative (quadratic) gravity
concepts.definition: Gravity action with curvature-squared terms (C^2, R^2); the propagator falls off as 1/p^4, making the theory power-counting renormalizable (Stelle 1977), but the Weyl-squared term introduces a massive spin-2 state with wrong-sign kinetic term (ghost).

concepts.name: Ostrogradsky ghost
concepts.definition: Generic instability of theories with more than two time derivatives: the Hamiltonian is unbounded below classically, and upon quantization the extra degree of freedom carries negative norm/energy, violating unitarity. The central obstruction for higher-derivative gravity.

concepts.name: Agravity (adimensional gravity)
concepts.definition: Salvio-Strumia scale-invariant gravity with only dimensionless couplings and a four-derivative graviton kinetic term; renormalizable and asymptotically free with no input mass scale, at the price of a physical ghost; predicts inflation from beta-functions.

concepts.name: Horava-Lifshitz gravity
concepts.definition: Gravity with anisotropic (Lifshitz) scaling t->b^z t, x->b x with z=3 in the UV, giving propagators ~1/(omega^2 - k^6) and power-counting renormalizability at the cost of breaking Lorentz invariance, which must re-emerge in the IR.

concepts.name: UV Lorentz-invariance violation
concepts.definition: Breaking of Lorentz symmetry at high momenta (as in Horava-Lifshitz), allowing improved UV behavior; radiative corrections threaten to leak the violation to low energies (tightly experimentally bounded), requiring fine-tuning, and a scalar mode suffers strong coupling.

concepts.name: Nonlocal / infinite-derivative ghost-free gravity
concepts.definition: Gravity whose kinetic operator contains an entire (root-free) function of the d'Alembertian, e.g. exp(-Box/M_s^2), exponentially suppressing the UV propagator and improving loop convergence without introducing new poles (no new ghosts); price is nonlocality.

concepts.name: Conformal (Weyl) gravity
concepts.definition: Gravity with action proportional to the Weyl tensor squared, invariant under local conformal rescalings; renormalizable but contains an Ostrogradsky ghost. In critical gravity / Maldacena's construction the massive ghost can be removed by boundary conditions.

concepts.name: Unimodular gravity
concepts.definition: Gravity with the constraint sqrt(-g)=const, so the cosmological constant appears as an integration constant rather than a Lagrangian coupling, offering a new vantage on the cosmological-constant problem; classically equivalent to GR, with subtle quantum (in)equivalence.

concepts.name: Shape dynamics
concepts.definition: Reformulation of GR (Barbour, Gomes, Koslowski) that trades spacetime refoliation invariance for 3D local conformal (scale) invariance as a gauge symmetry, realizing a relational, Machian description of gravitational dynamics.

concepts.name: Fakeons / purely virtual particles
concepts.definition: Anselmi's prescription quantizing would-be ghosts as fake, strictly off-shell degrees of freedom that never appear as asymptotic in/out states; this preserves unitarity (in the limit of vanishing cosmological constant) while keeping renormalizability of quadratic gravity.

concepts.name: BPS-protected counterterm operators
concepts.definition: Supersymmetric invariants built from shortened (BPS) superconformal multiplets that could serve as candidate counterterms; in N=8 supergravity these BPS invariants fail to be E7(7)-invariant, removing protected counterterms below seven loops.

concepts.name: Spectral dimension
concepts.definition: Effective dimension probed by a diffusion process on spacetime; Horava-Lifshitz gravity (like CDT) flows from d_s=4 in the IR to d_s=2 in the UV, an indicator of improved UV behavior shared across several quantum-gravity approaches.

formulas.name: Superficial degree of divergence in gravity
formulas.meaning: The superficial degree of divergence of an L-loop amplitude grows with loop order in d=4, so each loop needs counterterms of higher curvature dimension, making Einstein gravity perturbatively non-renormalizable.

formulas.name: Goroff-Sagnotti two-loop counterterm
formulas.meaning: Nonzero two-loop divergence of pure gravity (coefficient 209/2880, Weyl tensor cubed) that cannot be removed by field redefinition; the definitive proof of perturbative non-renormalizability.

formulas.name: Quantum-corrected Newtonian potential
formulas.meaning: EFT result for the gravitational potential: the second term is the classical post-Newtonian correction, the third (coefficient 41/(10 pi)) is the universal genuine quantum correction proportional to hbar.

formulas.name: Stelle quadratic-gravity action
formulas.meaning: Curvature-squared action whose 1/p^4 propagator makes gravity renormalizable; the Weyl-squared term carries a massive spin-2 ghost.

formulas.name: Higher-derivative propagator pole decomposition
formulas.meaning: Partial-fraction form showing the negative-residue pole at p^2=-M^2: a massive spin-2 ghost responsible for the unitarity problem of quadratic gravity.

formulas.name: Horava-Lifshitz action (z=3)
formulas.latex: S_{\rm HL} = \frac{1}{2\kappa^2}\int dt\,d^3x\,\sqrt{g}\,N\left[K_{ij}K^{ij} - \lambda K^2 - \mathcal{V}[g_{ij}]\right],\quad \mathcal{V}\supset M^{-4}(\nabla R)^2
formulas.meaning: Anisotropic gravity action with up to sixth-order spatial derivatives (z=3); the propagator ~1/(omega^2 - k^6) gives power-counting renormalizability at the cost of Lorentz violation.

formulas.name: Ghost-free nonlocal form factor
formulas.meaning: Infinite-derivative kinetic operator built from an entire function with no zeros; it exponentially suppresses the UV propagator without adding new poles, so no new ghosts appear.

formulas.name: N=8 critical dimension vs loop order
formulas.meaning: Dimension below which the L-loop four-point N=8 amplitude is finite. The relation D_c=4+6/L (matching the N=4 SYM bound) holds through four loops. At five loops the measured critical dimension is D_c=24/5, BELOW the 26/5 the formula would give: Bern et al. (2018) show this 24/5 is exactly what the D^8 R^4 counterterm and standard E7(7)/supersymmetry arguments predict, i.e. at five loops N=8 shows NO enhanced cancellation and behaves like N=4 SYM. D=4 finiteness is first threatened at L=7 (E7(7)-invariant partial^6 R^4).

formulas.name: KLT / double-copy relation
formulas.latex: M_n^{\rm tree} = \sum_{\sigma,\tau} A_n^{\rm tree}(\sigma)\,S[\sigma|\tau]\,\tilde A_n^{\rm tree}(\tau)
formulas.meaning: Gravity amplitudes are a double copy of two gauge-theory amplitudes joined by the KLT kernel S; N=8 SUGRA = (N=4 SYM)^2, enabling multiloop UV computations.

formulas.name: BCJ color-kinematics duality
formulas.latex: c_i + c_j + c_k = 0 \;\Longrightarrow\; n_i + n_j + n_k = 0
formulas.meaning: Kinematic numerators n_i can be arranged to satisfy the same Jacobi identities as the color factors c_i; replacing color by a second kinematic factor yields the gravity (double-copy) integrand.

formulas.name: One-loop pure-gravity counterterm
formulas.meaning: The one-loop divergence of pure gravity is proportional to R^2 and Ricci-squared, both of which vanish on-shell (R_{mu nu}=0), so pure gravity is one-loop finite; adding matter spoils this.

formulas.name: E7(7) single-soft-scalar constraint on counterterms
formulas.meaning: A counterterm is E7(7)-invariant iff the amplitude with one soft scalar (Goldstone of broken E7(7)) vanishes; D^4R^4 and D^6R^4 have nonzero soft limits, so no E7(7)-invariant counterterm exists below seven loops.

formulas.name: N=8 supermultiplet spin content
formulas.meaning: Binomial coefficients give the field content: 1 graviton, 8 gravitinos, 28 vectors, 56 fermions, 70 scalars (256 states total); the 70 scalars parametrize the coset E7(7)/SU(8).

formulas.name: EFT effective action of gravity
formulas.latex: S_{\rm eff} = \int d^4x\sqrt{-g}\left[\Lambda + \frac{R}{16\pi G} + c_1 R^2 + c_2 R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu} + \frac{d_1}{M^2}R^3 + \dots\right]
formulas.meaning: Derivative (energy) expansion of the gravitational effective action; unknown UV physics is absorbed into Wilson coefficients c_i, d_i while nonanalytic loop contributions give universal IR predictions, making GR predictive despite non-renormalizability.

references.authors: G. 't Hooft, M. Veltman
references.title: One-loop divergencies in the theory of gravitation
references.significance: Showed pure gravity is one-loop on-shell finite but diverges once matter is added; opened the question of perturbative renormalizability.

references.authors: D. Z. Freedman, P. van Nieuwenhuizen, S. Ferrara
references.title: Progress toward a theory of supergravity
references.significance: Construction of the first N=1 supergravity in 4D; founding paper of the supergravity program (Breakthrough Prize 2019).

references.authors: K. S. Stelle
references.title: Renormalization of higher-derivative quantum gravity
references.significance: Proved that curvature-squared gravity is renormalizable in 4D, at the cost of a massive spin-2 ghost; the canonical reference for higher-derivative gravity.

references.authors: E. Cremmer, B. Julia, J. Scherk
references.title: Supergravity theory in eleven dimensions
references.significance: Unique maximal 11D supergravity, the low-energy limit of M-theory and the parent of 4D N=8 supergravity.

references.authors: M. H. Goroff, A. Sagnotti
references.title: The ultraviolet behavior of Einstein gravity
references.significance: Computed the nonzero two-loop R^3 divergence (coefficient 209/2880), proving the perturbative non-renormalizability of pure gravity.

references.authors: A. E. M. van de Ven
references.title: Two-loop quantum gravity
references.significance: Independent confirmation of the Goroff-Sagnotti two-loop divergence.

references.authors: J. F. Donoghue
references.title: General relativity as an effective field theory: the leading quantum corrections
references.significance: Founding paper of gravity-as-EFT; showed reliable low-energy quantum predictions exist independent of UV completion.

references.authors: N. E. J. Bjerrum-Bohr, J. F. Donoghue, B. R. Holstein
references.title: Quantum gravitational corrections to the nonrelativistic scattering potential of two masses
references.significance: Established the universal 41/(10 pi) coefficient of the leading quantum correction to the Newtonian potential.

references.authors: Z. Bern, L. J. Dixon, R. Roiban
references.title: Is N=8 supergravity ultraviolet finite?
references.significance: Posed the modern finiteness question; the no-triangle hypothesis shows N=8 and N=4 SYM share UV behavior at one loop.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. M. Carrasco, L. J. Dixon, H. Johansson, D. A. Kosower, R. Roiban
references.title: Three-loop superfiniteness of N=8 supergravity
references.significance: Demonstrated three-loop cancellations stronger than superspace power-counting predicts; first evidence of enhanced cancellations.

references.authors: M. B. Green, H. Ooguri, J. H. Schwarz
references.title: Decoupling Supergravity from the Superstring
references.significance: Argued N=8 supergravity cannot be decoupled from string theory for d>3, so even if UV-finite it is not a standalone quantum gravity.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. M. Carrasco, H. Johansson
references.title: New relations for gauge-theory amplitudes
references.significance: Introduced BCJ color-kinematics duality, the key to the double copy and to multiloop supergravity computations.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. M. Carrasco, L. J. Dixon, H. Johansson, R. Roiban
references.title: Manifest ultraviolet behavior for the three-loop four-point amplitude of N=8 supergravity
references.significance: Explicit three-loop integrand showing D_c=6, matching N=4 SYM UV behavior.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. M. Carrasco, H. Johansson
references.title: Perturbative quantum gravity as a double copy of gauge theory
references.significance: Established the loop-level double copy: gravity integrands obtained directly from BCJ-form gauge-theory numerators.

references.authors: N. Beisert, H. Elvang, D. Z. Freedman, M. Kiermaier, A. Morales, S. Stieberger
references.title: E7(7) constraints on counterterms in N=8 supergravity
references.significance: Proved no E7(7)-invariant candidate counterterm exists below seven loops, making L=7 the critical order in 4D.

references.authors: Z. Bern, S. Davies, T. Dennen, Y.-t. Huang
references.title: Absence of three-loop four-point divergences in N=4 supergravity
references.significance: Showed N=4 supergravity is unexpectedly finite at three loops, an early enhanced-cancellation example.

references.authors: Z. Bern, S. Davies, T. Dennen, A. V. Smirnov, V. A. Smirnov
references.title: Ultraviolet properties of N=4 supergravity at four loops
references.significance: First UV divergence in a pure ungauged 4D supergravity, attributed to a U(1) duality anomaly.

references.authors: Z. Bern, S. Davies, T. Dennen
references.title: Enhanced ultraviolet cancellations in N=5 supergravity at four loops
references.significance: N=5 is finite at four loops despite expectations, defining the notion of enhanced cancellation.

references.authors: A. Salvio, A. Strumia
references.title: Agravity
references.significance: Dimensionless, renormalizable, asymptotically free gravity with no input scale; revived the ghost debate and links gravity to inflation.

references.authors: R. Kallosh
references.title: An update on perturbative N=8 supergravity
references.significance: Pure-spinor argument for possible all-loop finiteness; highlights the conflict over a putative seven-loop divergence.

references.authors: A. O. Barvinsky, D. Blas, M. Herrero-Valea, S. M. Sibiryakov, C. F. Steinwachs
references.title: Renormalization of Horava gravity
references.significance: Proof of perturbative renormalizability of projectable Horava-Lifshitz gravity, a concrete renormalizable quantum gravity.

references.authors: H. Gies, B. Knorr, S. Lippoldt, F. Saueressig
references.title: Gravitational two-loop counterterm is asymptotically safe
references.significance: Connects the Goroff-Sagnotti counterterm to a nontrivial fixed point, bridging perturbative gravity and asymptotic safety.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. Carrasco, W.-M. Chen, A. Edison, H. Johansson, J. Parra-Martinez, R. Roiban, M. Zeng
references.title: Ultraviolet properties of N=8 supergravity at five loops
references.significance: State-of-the-art five-loop result: critical dimension D_c=24/5 with a D^8 R^4 counterterm, the most advanced explicit N=8 UV computation.

references.authors: P. Horava
references.title: Quantum gravity at a Lifshitz point
references.significance: Introduced anisotropic z=3 gravity, power-counting renormalizable via Lorentz violation in the UV.

references.authors: T. Biswas, E. Gerwick, T. Koivisto, A. Mazumdar
references.title: Towards singularity- and ghost-free theories of gravity
references.significance: General infinite-derivative (nonlocal) ghost-free gravity action with improved UV behavior and resolved singularities.

references.authors: J. Maldacena
references.title: Einstein gravity from conformal gravity
references.significance: Showed conformal gravity reduces to Einstein gravity with the massive ghost removed by boundary conditions; links to holography.

references.authors: J. F. Donoghue
references.title: Quantum general relativity and effective field theory
references.significance: Modern review of gravity-as-EFT, the consensus IR baseline that any quantum-gravity theory must reproduce.

references.authors: Z. Bern, J. J. Carrasco, M. Chiodaroli, H. Johansson, R. Roiban
references.title: Supergravity amplitudes, the double copy and ultraviolet behavior
references.significance: Authoritative 2023 review of the amplitudes/double-copy approach to supergravity UV behavior and the open finiteness question.

references.authors: D. Anselmi
references.title: Quantum gravity with purely virtual particles from asymptotically local quantum field theory
references.significance: Latest fakeon-based proposal for unitary, renormalizable quantum gravity that quantizes the quadratic-gravity ghost as a purely virtual particle.

references.authors: Quanta Magazine (C. Wood)
references.title: Old 'ghost' theory of quantum gravity makes a comeback
references.significance: Documents the 2025 revival of quadratic gravity and competing ghost resolutions (Donoghue, Buoninfante, Salvio, Holdom, Anselmi vs Platania).

openProblems.statement: Is N=8 supergravity ultraviolet finite in D=4 to all loop orders? The critical test is the seventh loop, where an E7(7)-invariant partial^6 R^4 candidate counterterm first becomes admissible.
openProblems.whyHard: An explicit seven-loop four-point computation is far beyond current technology (the five-loop result already required generalized double copy and cutting-edge integral reduction), and the observed enhanced cancellations have no symmetry explanation to extrapolate from.
openProblems.attempts: Explicit computations to five loops (D_c=24/5), E7(7) counterterm analyses, pure-spinor arguments (Kallosh: no divergence beyond six loops; earlier pure-spinor work: divergence at seven loops). No consensus.

openProblems.statement: What is the mechanism behind enhanced UV cancellations seen in N=5 (four loops) and N=8 (three to five loops), which exceed all known symmetry and superspace power-counting predictions?
openProblems.whyHard: It appears to require an as-yet-unidentified symmetry or nonlocal structure; without it, results cannot be extrapolated to higher loops or used to settle finiteness.
openProblems.attempts: Duality-symmetry (E7(7), U(1)) analyses, supersymmetric decomposition of amplitudes, double-copy structural studies; partial explanations only (e.g. N=5 via duality anomaly absence).

openProblems.statement: Can the massive spin-2 Ostrogradsky ghost of renormalizable higher-derivative (Stelle/conformal/agravity) gravity be made compatible with unitarity and stability?
openProblems.whyHard: Renormalizability requires a 1/p^4 propagator that necessarily introduces a wrong-sign pole; reconciling this with positive norms and causal stability is a deep structural tension.
openProblems.attempts: Fakeons / purely virtual particles (Anselmi), PT-symmetry, modified probability rules (Salvio, Holdom), short-distance-only instability with statistical macro-causality (Donoghue, Menezes); all contested (Platania).

openProblems.statement: Does Horava-Lifshitz gravity recover Lorentz invariance in the IR without fine-tuning and avoid strong coupling of the extra scalar mode?
openProblems.whyHard: Radiative corrections leak UV Lorentz violation into low energies (tightly bounded experimentally), demanding extreme fine-tuning; the scalar graviton mode becomes strongly coupled near the GR limit.
openProblems.attempts: Detailed-balance and projectable/non-projectable variants, proof of renormalizability (Barvinsky et al. 2016), RG analyses; strong-coupling problem persists.

openProblems.statement: Is unimodular gravity quantum-mechanically equivalent to general relativity, and does it genuinely improve the cosmological-constant problem?
openProblems.whyHard: Whether Lambda as an integration constant (rather than a coupling) escapes radiative corrections is regularization- and measure-dependent; arguments on both sides are subtle.
openProblems.attempts: Path-integral and renormalization-group analyses, comparisons of effective actions; results suggest equivalence up to high momenta but the cosmological-constant payoff is disputed.

openProblems.statement: Do infinite-derivative ghost-free (nonlocal) gravity theories admit a consistent, causal initial-value problem and a unitary S-matrix?
openProblems.whyHard: Infinitely many derivatives obscure the notion of initial data and analytic continuation of the nonlocal propagator (contour choice) is ambiguous, threatening causality.
openProblems.attempts: Entire-function form factors e^{-Box/M_s^2}, prescriptions for the nonlocal propagator, explicit UV finiteness checks; full S-matrix consistency not proven.

openProblems.statement: Does perturbatively finite N=8 supergravity belong to the swampland (no consistent UV completion as a standalone theory of quantum gravity)?
openProblems.whyHard: Green-Ooguri-Schwarz show it cannot decouple from strings for d>3, but a formal proof via modern swampland criteria, and the role of nonperturbative effects, are missing.
openProblems.attempts: Nondecoupling argument (towers of KK/winding/brane states), swampland-distance and weak-gravity heuristics; no rigorous swampland classification.

openProblems.statement: If N=8 supergravity is perturbatively finite, what does this imply about the relationship between UV finiteness and UV completeness (a finite theory need not be a complete theory of quantum gravity)?
openProblems.whyHard: Finiteness and completeness are logically distinct; black-hole/nonperturbative physics may obstruct consistency even for a perturbatively finite theory, and there is no theorem connecting the two.
openProblems.attempts: Conceptual analyses, string-decoupling arguments, swampland reasoning; remains a foundational open question.

connections.description: N=8 supergravity is the low-energy limit of type II superstrings on T^6 and 11D supergravity is the low-energy limit of M-theory. Green-Ooguri-Schwarz (2007) proved N=8 SUGRA cannot be decoupled from strings for d>3, so string theory is its UV completion. The double copy/KLT explicitly relates closed strings (gravity) to open strings (gauge).

All multiloop progress on N=8 UV behavior relies on amplitude technology: generalized unitarity, BCJ color-kinematics duality, the double copy and the no-triangle hypothesis. The KLT kernel and kinematic numerators are the shared mathematical substrate.

Both target the non-renormalizability of gravity but differently: asymptotic safety seeks a nonperturbative UV fixed point while supergravity relies on perturbative cancellations. Gies-Knorr-Lippoldt-Saueressig (2016) showed the Goroff-Sagnotti two-loop counterterm sits at a fixed point, and Stelle's higher-derivative gravity feeds asymptotic-safety truncations.

Donoghue's EFT of gravity is the universal low-energy/semiclassical baseline: the quantum correction to the Newtonian potential (coefficient 41/(10 pi)) and other nonanalytic terms must be reproduced by any quantum-gravity theory, providing a mandatory IR consistency check.

The distinction between UV finiteness and UV completeness, the meaning of non-renormalizability, and the ghost/unitarity tension in higher-derivative gravity are central conceptual issues that the EFT viewpoint reframes (predictivity without renormalizability).

Conformal (Weyl) gravity and critical gravity arise in renormalized AdS actions and boundary holographic descriptions; Maldacena's removal of the conformal-gravity ghost uses boundary conditions, and color-kinematics/double copy is being studied in curved (AdS) backgrounds.

Horava-Lifshitz gravity shares with CDT a preferred foliation (causal/preferred time) and UV spectral-dimension reduction d_s -> 2, suggesting a common UV structure. The formal link between the CDT continuum limit and a Horava fixed point is essentially unexplored.

Green-Ooguri-Schwarz conjecture that perturbatively finite N=8 supergravity is in the swampland because it cannot decouple from strings (infinite towers of KK/winding/brane states). A formal proof via modern swampland criteria (distance, weak-gravity) is missing.

Unimodular gravity recasts the cosmological constant as an integration constant rather than a Lagrangian coupling, potentially shielding it from radiative corrections; whether this genuinely addresses the cosmological-constant problem at the quantum level is contested.

Nonlocal/infinite-derivative ghost-free gravity regularizes the UV via a fundamental nonlocality scale M_s with form factor e^{-Box/M_s^2}, analogous to noncommutative 'smearing' of spacetime points. The relationship is analogical and almost unexplored.

Horava-Lifshitz z=3 scaling yields scale-invariant primordial perturbations without inflation; agravity predicts inflation (n_s ~ 0.967, r ~ 0.13) from beta-functions; unimodular gravity changes the status of Lambda in cosmology. Explicit per-theory, not unified.

Almost no direct bridge: LQG is nonperturbative and background-independent, supergravity/EFT perturbative and background-dependent. Shape dynamics (conformal reformulation of GR) has a distant kinship with relational/problem-of-time aspects of LQG, but no concrete link to UV behavior exists.

Horava-Lifshitz gravity exhibits a UV spectral dimension d_s -> 2 (from d_s=4 in the IR), the same dimensional-reduction signature found in CDT, asymptotic safety and other approaches; this shared UV indicator is a candidate deep cross-approach structure but is not quantitatively connected to the supergravity cancellation mechanism.

Some Lifshitz/Horava models were proposed as a 'Fermi-point scenario of emergent gravity' (emergent Lorentz symmetry from a Fermi point); agravity and Stelle gravity present Einstein gravity as IR-emergent from a dimensionless UV theory, with Newton's constant generated by dimensional transmutation from beta-functions. Conceptual bridge only, not formalized.

A UV-finite or UV-complete quantum gravity must consistently describe black holes and the information paradox; the Green-Ooguri-Schwarz nondecoupling of N=8 from strings relies on black-hole/brane states. Whether perturbative finiteness says anything about black-hole microstates is open. The classical double copy (Kerr-Schild, gravitational waves) may link perturbative amplitudes to black-hole physics.

EFT amplitudes in the classical limit yield post-Newtonian/post-Minkowskian coefficients for binary inspirals (gravitational-wave 'amplitudes for LIGO/LISA' program). Universal quantum corrections (light bending, Shapiro delay, 41/(10 pi) potential) are computed but far below experimental sensitivity; the bridge to measuring quantum gravity via EFT is conceptually clear but practically out of reach.
